method_summary: A head-to-head comparison of two agentic AI systems (Claude Code and Codex) in performing a complete scientific analysis pipeline on simulated gravitational wave data, from signal recovery to manuscript generation.
key_innovation: The first direct, controlled comparison of agentic AI systems on a realistic scientific computing workflow, revealing critical differences in behavior, speed, and error handling.
datasets_used: Simulated binary black hole signal injections for the Einstein Telescope

key_results.scientific_convergence: Both runs converged on the same scientific results

best_result: Claude Code completed the pipeline significantly faster (~3.4 min vs ~16 min) while achieving scientific convergence with Codex.
limitations_stated: Agents exhibited substantially different behaviors and computational costs
Claude Code made silent deviations from the specification
A subtle difference in SNR interpretation led to genuine scientific divergence in one run
title: First head-to-head comparison of agentic AI applied to the analysis of simulated data of the Einstein Telescope
